prd.name: Rich PRD Content (XHTML)

requirement.name: Rich-text fields and authoring form
requirement.description: A fixed set of "rich-text" elements may contain inline XHTML in addition to plain text. Authors write XHTML directly; CDATA is supported as a fallback when content can't easily be made well-formed.
requirement.rule: The rich-text fields are exactly: <overview>, <description> (inside <requirement>), the text content of <rule>, the text content of <current> / <expected> / <steps> inside <bug>, and the text content of <finding>. Every other element accepts plain text only.

Authors write inline XHTML directly inside a rich-text field, with no wrapper. Content must be well-formed XML: self-closing void tags (
, ,
), explicit close tags for every element, and & escaped as &. The XML parser treats child elements as element nodes, not strings.

The validator accepts CDATA-wrapped content on input as an authoring convenience — for example, when pasting raw HTML from another tool. prd format normalizes CDATA to XHTML on the next run; round-tripping a CDATA-wrapped field through prd format produces an XHTML form, so any markup inside CDATA must itself be well-formed XHTML (otherwise it is escaped to literal text).
A rich-text field containing only plain text (no child elements, no CDATA) continues to validate and render exactly as before. Existing PRDs need no migration.

requirement.name: Validation and formatting
requirement.description: prd validate and prd format understand rich-text fields and pass their inner content through without imposing structural rules on HTML descendants.
requirement.rule: When prd validate enters a rich-text field, it stops applying child-tag, ordering, and required-attribute checks to descendants of that field. Any XHTML tag is allowed at any depth inside a rich-text field.
prd format preserves XHTML child elements verbatim — tag name, attributes, attribute order, and nesting are emitted as authored. It does not re-wrap inline XHTML in CDATA.

prd format does not preserve CDATA wrappers on output. On read, CDATA contents are treated as element text; on write, that text is emitted as XHTML with & / < / > escaping. CDATA is therefore an author-time convenience only.
Running prd format twice produces byte-identical output on the second pass. The first pass over a CDATA-authored field rewrites it to XHTML; the second pass is a no-op.

requirement.name: JSON API serialization
requirement.description: The dashboard's JSON API exposes rich-text fields as opaque HTML strings so the frontend doesn't need to know which authoring form was used.
requirement.rule: In GET /api/prd/{module}/{feature} and GET /api/index responses, every rich-text field appears as a single HTML string field (description, overview, rule.text, bug.current, finding.text, …). The string is the inner content of the XML element: text and serialized child elements concatenated in source order.

When the source used XHTML child elements, those elements are serialized into the HTML string with stable attribute order and proper escaping of text content. Self-closing void tags remain self-closing.
When the source used CDATA, the JSON string contains the raw inner content without the <![CDATA[ / ]]> markers.
When the source contained only plain text, the JSON string is exactly that text. No HTML tags are introduced.

requirement.name: Images: local assets and external URLs
requirement.description: Authors embed images via . Local image files live alongside the PRD on disk; external images are referenced by URL. The dashboard serves local images from a dedicated endpoint with strict path-traversal protection.
requirement.rule: A PRD at prd//.xml may reference local images with relative src paths. The path is resolved against prd// so resolves to prd//screenshots/login-error.png.
An whose src is an absolute URL (http, https, or data:) is passed through to the dashboard unchanged. No server-side fetch.

The dashboard exposes GET /api/prd-asset/{module}/{feature}/{path} which serves the file at <prd_dir>/{module}/{path}. The endpoint resolves the path and rejects any request that escapes <prd_dir>/{module}/ with HTTP 404, including symlinks that point outside.
Before rendering rich-text HTML, the dashboard rewrites every relative (no scheme, not starting with /) to /api/prd-asset/{module}/{feature}/{src}. Absolute and data URLs are left alone.

requirement.name: Cross-references between PRDs
requirement.description: A prd: URI scheme lets one PRD link to another PRD, optionally to a specific requirement, rule, or bug anchor. See dashboard/viewer#R2 for an example of a sidebar rule that points back at a discovery rule via this scheme.
requirement.rule: In the dashboard, clicking a prd: link does not perform a browser navigation. Instead a client-side handler parses the URI, calls react-router to navigate to /p/{module}/{feature}, and if a fragment is present scrolls the matching element into view.

When the prd skill loads a PRD, after it presents its primary summary it lists any prd: cross-references it found, grouped by target PRD, so the user can decide whether to load those PRDs next. The skill does not auto-load referenced PRDs.

requirement.name: Dashboard rendering
requirement.description: The dashboard renders rich-text HTML in both themes with prose typography, and supports the same set of tags the API delivers without sanitization.
requirement.rule: A single <RichContent html={...} module={module} feature={feature} /> React component is the only renderer for rich-text strings. It is used wherever the old code rendered {rule.text}, {req.description}, {f.text}, {bug.current/expected/steps}, and {prd.overview}.
RichContent uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML with no sanitizer. The skill doc warns that opening an untrusted PRD in the dashboard is equivalent to running its HTML in your browser.
Rich content uses a .prd-prose wrapper so headings, paragraphs, lists, blockquotes, inline code, code blocks, and tables render with consistent typography in both light and dark themes.
Images rendered inside rich content are bounded to the column width by default (max-width: 100%; height: auto) so a wide screenshot doesn't blow out the layout.

requirement.name: Skill awareness
requirement.description: The `prd` skill teaches Claude how rich content is authored and how to consume it when loading a PRD.
requirement.rule: skills/prd/SKILL.md documents the rich-text field list, XHTML-first authoring rules (well-formedness, void tags, & escaping), the CDATA fallback, the prd: cross-ref scheme, and the local-vs-external image rules.
When the skill loads a PRD, for every relative in a rich-text field it Reads the resolved local file so the image is available to Claude as visual context. Absolute URLs are not fetched.
The skill's context summary includes a Cross-references section listing every prd: link found in the PRD, with target module/feature and (if present) the requirement/rule/bug anchor.

requirement.name: Backwards compatibility
requirement.description: All existing PRDs, CLI behavior, JSON-API shape for plain-text fields, and skill behavior continue to work unchanged.
requirement.rule: Every PRD currently under prd/ validates, formats, and renders identically after this change, with no edits to the file.
prd validate, prd format, and prd stats keep their current command-line interface and exit codes.
The JSON keys returned by /api/index and /api/prd/{module}/{feature} are the same as before; only their values may now be HTML strings instead of plain text.
